mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator allows you to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters on Kubernetes. It simplifies the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://charts.rabbitmq.com

helm repo update

```

> This adds the RabbitMQ Helm chart repository.
> This updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the deployment of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq --create-namespace

```

> This installs the operator in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined configuration file.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> This applies the RabbitMQ cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> This lists all pods in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not managed properly.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq

```

> This uninstalls the RabbitMQ operator.
> This deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl uncordon <pod-name>

```

> This drains the RabbitMQ pods before the upgrade.
> This upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Replace <pod-name> with the name of the pod you want to uncordon after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on RabbitMQ pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the RabbitMQ pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to RabbitMQ server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the RabbitMQ service is not reachable or not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the RabbitMQ service is running and check the service endpoints using 'kubectl get svc -n rabbitmq'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the RabbitMQ pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in your RabbitMQ deployment configuration or add more nodes to your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied' when accessing RabbitMQ
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the RBAC permissions are not correctly set for the RabbitMQ service account.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct RBAC roles and bindings are applied for the RabbitMQ service account.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, added new monitoring features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods in a 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
